author: CLEMENT OF ROME

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: Chapter XXXI.—Let us see by what means we may obtain the divine blessing.

ccel.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

source.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

title: Chapter XXXI.—Let us see by what means we may obtain the divine blessing.

source.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

verses.text: Let us cleave then to His blessing, and consider what are the means of possessing it.
Let us think over the things which have taken place from the beginning.
For what reason was our father Abraham blessed? was it not because he wrought righteousness and truth through faith?
Isaac, with perfect confidence, as if knowing what was to happen, cheerfully yielded himself as a sacrifice.
Jacob, through reason of his brother, went forth with humility from his own land, and came to Laban and served him; and there was given to him the sceptre of the twelve tribes of Israel.
